description: Fix XSS vulnerabilities: The sanitize.ts functions (sanitizeHtmlContent and sanitizeCodeHighlight) return unsanitized HTML when typeof window === 'undefined' (SSR). CodeQL flags dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage because DOMPurify is bypassed during SSR. Fix by using a server-safe sanitization approach (e.g., isomorphic-dompurify or a regex-based server-side strip for dangerous patterns).
steps: In packages/shared-ui/src/utils/sanitize.ts, replace the SSR bypass (returning raw html) with server-safe sanitization. Use the 'isomorphic-dompurify' package OR implement server-side sanitization that strips script tags, event handlers, and dangerous protocols when window is undefined. The key issue is the `if (typeof window === 'undefined') return html;` pattern which returns unsanitized content.

Install isomorphic-dompurify in the shared-ui package (pnpm add isomorphic-dompurify --filter @quant/shared-ui). Replace the DOMPurify import with isomorphic-dompurify. This makes DOMPurify work in both Node.js and browser contexts, eliminating the need for the SSR bypass.
Remove the `if (typeof window === 'undefined') return html;` check from both functions since isomorphic-dompurify handles both environments.
Update the test file packages/shared-ui/src/__tests__/sanitize.test.ts to verify that sanitization works in both environments (no longer returning raw input when window is undefined).
Verify: run pnpm --filter @quant/shared-ui test to ensure sanitize tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: sanitizeHtmlContent strips <script> tags and event handlers regardless of environment (SSR or client)

sanitizeCodeHighlight only allows with class and
tags regardless of environment
No code path exists that returns unsanitized HTML
findings: Replaced dompurify with isomorphic-dompurify. Removed SSR bypass. All 257 tests in shared-ui pass (including 11 sanitize tests). Typecheck has pre-existing TS6305 errors from unbuilt brand/common package dist outputs (confirmed same on base branch). The @types/dompurify package shows a deprecation warning but still works correctly with isomorphic-dompurify.
